title: Using Direct Objects - Lo and La
description: Learn to use 'lo' and 'la' for direct objects and formal address, and form questions in Spanish.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: What was the word for 'it', 'him', or 'you' (formal)?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Lo. To make it feminine, change the 'o' to 'a', giving us la. This means 'her', 'it' (for feminine nouns), or 'you' (formal, feminine). For example, 'casa' (house) is feminine. How would you say 'I want to see it', referring to the house?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Quiero verla. This could also mean 'I want to see her'. 'La' could mean 'her', 'it', or 'you' (formal, feminine). The use of formal language varies by region, so it's safe to start formally with 'lo' or 'la'. 'Quiere' means 'he wants', 'she wants', 'it wants', or 'you (formal) want'. How would you say 'you want to visit me'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Quiere visitarme. To make it a question, just change your intonation. How would you say 'Do you want to visit me?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿Quiere visitarme? How would you say 'You don't want to visit me'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No quiere visitarme. Now, how would you ask 'Don't you want to visit me?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿No quiere visitarme? You can add 'why' with 'por qué'. How would you ask 'Why don't you want to visit me?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿Por qué no quiere visitarme? The verb 'to come' is 'venir'. How would you say 'Do you want to come?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿Quiere venir? 'Venir' is related to 'bienvenido' (welcome). How would you say 'Do you want to come to the house?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿Quiere venir a la casa? How about 'Do you want to come to visit me?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿Quiere venir a visitarme? Now, 'Don't you want to come to visit me?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿No quiere venir a visitarme? How would you ask 'Why don't you want to come to visit me?'

turns.speaker: student
expected_answers: por qué no quiere venir a visitarme?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿Por qué no quiere venir a visitarme? The word 'debo' means 'I owe' or 'I must'. How would you say 'I must continue'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. How would you say 'I must identify it'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Debo identificarlo. For feminine objects, what change would you make?

outro.text: You've learned how to use 'lo' and 'la' for direct objects and formal address in Spanish.
Try forming questions using 'quieres' and 'por qué'. Practice with different verbs and objects.
Remember, intonation is key for forming questions in Spanish. Practice changing statements into questions.
